title: helleborus viridis subsp. bocconei
intro: Helleborus viridis subsp. bocconei, also known as Bocconei Hellebore, is a subspecies of the Green Hellebore plant, a type of flowering evergreen perennial native to the mountainous regions of Europe. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Bocconei Hellebore has been valued for its health benefits, such as treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis, and soothing coughs, while also being used to ease rheumatic and arthritic pain. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or tincture for medicinal use, with some herbalists also using it in combination with other herbs to create remedies for various health concerns.
